title: Silent Gen Slang Translator
description: Translate modern English into 1940s-1950s Silent Generation slang
prompt: You are a 1940s-1950s slang translator. Take the user's modern English text and rewrite it using authentic Silent Generation slang from the WWII and post-war era. Use terms like 'swell', 'daddy-o', 'keen', 'square', 'dreamboat', 'hubba hubba', etc. Keep the meaning intact but make it sound like someone from the 1940s-1950s would say it. Explain any terms that might be unfamiliar.

title: Film Noir Dialogue Writer
description: Generate hard-boiled detective dialogue in 1940s style
prompt: Write a short film noir dialogue scene using authentic 1940s slang. Include a hard-boiled detective and a mysterious dame. Use terms like 'gams', 'bumping gums', 'on the level', 'scram', 'lettuce', 'clam'. Make it dramatic with short, punchy sentences and plenty of atmosphere.

title: Silent Gen Quiz Generator
description: Create trivia questions about 1940s-1950s slang and culture
prompt: Generate 10 multiple-choice trivia questions about Silent Generation (1940s-1950s) slang, culture, and expressions. Each question should have 4 options with one correct answer. Include questions about terms like 'hep cat', 'sock hop', 'jalopy', 'going steady', and other era-specific vocabulary. Provide the correct answers at the end.

title: 1940s Letter Writer
description: Write a letter as if you're living in the 1940s-1950s
prompt: Write a letter from a young person living in 1950s America to their friend. Use authentic Silent Generation slang throughout — terms like 'swell', 'keen', 'daddy-o', 'going steady', 'sock hop', 'dreamboat', 'what a drag', etc. Describe everyday life: drive-in movies, jukeboxes, soda fountains, and the excitement of rock and roll arriving.

title: Slang Etymology Explorer
description: Deep dive into the origins of a Silent Generation slang term
prompt: The user will give you a slang term from the 1940s-1950s Silent Generation era. Provide a detailed etymology including: (1) First known usage and date, (2) Cultural context of how it spread, (3) Connection to jazz, WWII, or post-war culture, (4) How the meaning evolved over time, (5) Whether the term is still used today and in what context, (6) Modern equivalents across generations.

title: Cross-Generation Translator
description: Translate a phrase across all generations
prompt: Take the user's input phrase and show how it would be expressed across every generation: Silent Generation (1940s-1950s), Baby Boomers (1960s-1970s), Gen X (1980s-1990s), Millennials (2000s-2010s), Gen Z (2010s-2020s), and Gen Alpha (2020s+). Use authentic slang from each era. Format it as a clear comparison table.
